title: Example 8-7 shear joints with eccentric loading

notes: Example 8-7 style input. The bolt pattern and geometry are defined entirely in the JSON input.
The external load is 16 kN downward at x = 425 mm relative to the bolt-group centroid shown in the textbook figure.
The bolts are M16 and the threads are assumed not to extend into the joint, so the shank area is used for shear stress.
The maximum bearing stress is evaluated against the listed bearing thicknesses; the 10-mm channel web governs in the textbook example.
The critical bending section is modeled as a 15-by-200 mm rectangle with two 15-by-16 mm rectangular cutouts centered at y = ±60 mm.
